summary: IBM, in collaboration with researchers from The University of Manchester, Oxford University, ETH Zurich, EPFL, and the University of Regensburg, has synthesized and characterized the first molecule with a half-Möbius electronic topology, as published in Science on March 5, 2026. The molecule C₁₃Cl₂ was constructed atom-by-atom and its unique electronic behavior—marked by a corkscrew-like structure—was confirmed via quantum computing simulation using an IBM quantum computer. This achievement demonstrates deliberate engineering of electronic topology and quantum computing’s capacity to model complex, highly entangled molecular systems beyond classical computational capabilities. Dr. Igor Rončević highlighted the discovery as a breakthrough for controlling material properties via electronic topology, enabled by quantum-centric supercomputing.
